stories.title: Exclusion Filters for Saved Views and Filter Bar
stories.description:
Add "is not" (exclusion) filter support across all filter types — labels, status, priority, assignee, and projects — matching the pattern used by Linear, Notion, and ClickUp.

## Data Model
Add parallel `exclude*` arrays to `FilterConfig` and the labelStore:
- `excludeLabelIds?: string[]` / `excludeLabelFilters: Set<string>`
- `excludeStatusIds?: string[]` / `excludeStatusFilters: Set<string>`
- `excludePriorities?: number[]` / `excludePriorityFilters: Set<number>`
- `excludeAssigneeIds?: string[]` / `excludeAssigneeFilters: Set<string>`
- `excludeProjectIds?: string[]` / `excludeProjectFilters: Set<string>`

Existing saved views without `exclude*` fields load fine (backward compatible — missing fields = empty sets = no exclusions).

## UI Interaction
When the user opens a filter picker (e.g., Labels), there's an operator selector at the top: "is" (default) / "is not". Selecting "is not" and picking values adds them to the exclusion set. Both include and exclude rows coexist in the FilterBar:
```
Labels is:      ● Todoozy  ● Bug        [x]
Labels is not:  ● Later                  [x]
```
The "is not" text is styled in muted-red. Chips keep their normal styling — the operator text differentiates. Each row has its own clear button.

This pattern applies to ALL filter types that support the picker UI: labels, status, priority, assignee, projects.

## Conflict Resolution
Last action wins. If a value is in include and the user adds it to exclude, it moves to exclude (removed from include). And vice versa. No contradictions possible.

## Query Logic

### SQL (TaskRepository.search)
- Column filters (status, priority, assignee, project): `NOT IN (?, ?)` clause
- Label exclusion: subquery `AND t.id NOT IN (SELECT task_id FROM task_labels WHERE label_id IN (?, ?))`
- Add to `TaskSearchFilters` interface: `exclude_label_ids?: string[]`, `exclude_status_ids?: string[]`, `exclude_priorities?: number[]`, `exclude_assigned_to_ids?: string[]`, `exclude_project_ids?: string[]`

### Client-side (SavedViewListView)
- For each exclude filter set, if the task matches ANY excluded value, filter it out
- Label exclusion: `if ([...excludeLabelFilters].some(fid => labelIds.has(fid))) return false`
- Column exclusion: `if (excludeStatusFilters.has(task.status_id)) return false` etc.

## Saved Views
Serialize/deserialize the `exclude*` fields in `filter_config` JSON alongside existing include fields. Old views without them load without errors.

## MCP
Add exclusion params to `search_tasks` tool:
- `exclude_label_id: string` — exclude tasks with this label
- `exclude_status_id: string` — exclude tasks with this status
- `exclude_priority: number` — exclude tasks with this priority

## Views Affected
Exclusion filters must work in: TaskListView, MyDayView, SavedViewListView, CalendarView.

## Keyboard
Operator toggle ("is"/"is not") in the picker is focusable and keyboard-accessible (Tab, Enter/Space). No dedicated shortcuts.

## Key Files
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/labelStore.ts` — add exclude sets, toggle methods, serialization
- `src/renderer/src/shared/components/FilterBar.tsx` — operator selector in pickers, exclude row rendering
- `src/main/repositories/TaskRepository.ts` — add NOT IN clauses to search()
- `src/renderer/src/features/views/SavedViewListView.tsx` — client-side exclude filtering
- `src/renderer/src/features/views/MyDayView.tsx` — apply exclude filters
- `src/renderer/src/features/views/CalendarView.tsx` — apply exclude filters
- `src/main/mcp-server.ts` — add exclude params to search_tasks
- `src/shared/types.ts` — extend TaskSearchFilters

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: User can add 'is not' filters for labels, status, priority, assignee, and projects via the filter picker
Include and exclude rows display separately in the FilterBar with clear operator labels
'is not' text uses muted-red styling; chips retain normal styling
Adding a value to exclude removes it from include (and vice versa) — last action wins
Exclusion filters work in TaskListView, MyDayView, SavedViewListView, and CalendarView
Saved views persist exclusion filters and load them correctly
Existing saved views without exclusion fields load without errors
TaskRepository.search() supports exclude_label_ids, exclude_status_ids, exclude_priorities, exclude_assignee_ids, exclude_project_ids
MCP search_tasks tool supports exclude_label_id, exclude_status_id, exclude_priority params
Label exclusion uses subquery (NOT IN task_labels), column exclusions use NOT IN
All filter types are keyboard-accessible
Clearing a filter row clears only that row (include or exclude), not both
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Supabase Full Sync Engine
stories.description:
Extend the existing shared-project sync infrastructure to sync ALL data (personal and shared projects, settings, themes, saved views, areas) to Supabase automatically. SQLite remains the local source of truth. Every write goes to SQLite first, then pushes to Supabase in the background. Realtime subscriptions pull remote changes instantly. On new device login, all data is pulled from Supabase to populate the empty local database.

## Architecture
- Rename Supabase `shared_*` tables to generic names (`projects`, `tasks`, `statuses`, `project_members`, `activity_log`, `project_invites`) via Supabase migration
- Add new user-scoped tables: `user_settings`, `user_themes`, `user_saved_views`, `user_project_areas`, `user_labels` (with RLS: `user_id = auth.uid()`)
- Personal projects use the same `projects`/`tasks`/etc. tables — owner is inserted as sole member, existing RLS (project_members-based) works unchanged
- Update all SyncService references from `shared_*` → new table names

## Sync Flow
- **Automatic on login**: If `last_sync_at` is not set, batch-upload all local data to Supabase (upsert in chunks of 50-100). Show "Syncing your data..." progress indicator.
- **Ongoing**: Every local write → immediate push to Supabase. Realtime subscriptions for all projects deliver remote changes instantly.
- **New device**: Empty SQLite → pull everything from Supabase → populate local DB → set `last_sync_at` → subscribe to Realtime.
- **Deletes propagate**: Local deletes push to Supabase, Realtime DELETE events remove from local DB.

## Offline Behavior
- **Personal projects**: Continue working normally. Queue writes in `sync_queue`, flush when back online.
- **Shared projects**: Grey out / read-only with banner "You're offline — shared projects are read-only." No conflict resolution needed.

## Online/Offline Detection
- Primary: Supabase Realtime connection state (SUBSCRIBED = online, CLOSED/CHANNEL_ERROR = offline)
- Fallback: `navigator.onLine`
- On reconnect: flush sync_queue for personal projects, re-enable shared projects

## Conflict Resolution
Per-row last-write-wins using `updated_at`. Only applies to personal project offline queue flush — shared projects are read-only when offline so no conflicts.

## UI
- Sync status icon next to the ToDoozy header in top left corner
- Green dot: synced, spinning: syncing, orange: offline
- Click shows tooltip with last sync time
- Shared projects show "read-only" banner when offline
- First-time sync shows progress bar

## Supabase Changes
- Rename `shared_projects` → `projects`, `shared_tasks` → `tasks`, `shared_statuses` → `statuses`, `shared_project_members` → `project_members`, `shared_activity_log` → `activity_log`, `shared_project_invites` → `project_invites`
- Add tables: `user_settings(id, user_id, key, value, updated_at)`, `user_themes(id, user_id, ..., updated_at)`, `user_saved_views(id, user_id, ..., updated_at)`, `user_project_areas(id, user_id, ..., updated_at)`, `user_labels(id, user_id, name, color, ..., updated_at)`
- RLS on new tables: `user_id = auth.uid()`
- Existing RLS on renamed tables: no changes needed (still project_members based)

## Key Files
- `src/renderer/src/services/SyncService.ts` — extend for all projects + user-level data sync
- `src/renderer/src/services/PersonalSyncService.ts` — new, handles user-level data (settings, themes, views, areas, labels)
- `src/renderer/src/features/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` — sync status icon
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/syncStore.ts` — new Zustand store for sync state (online/offline, syncing, last_sync_at)
- All stores that write data (taskStore, labelStore, settingsStore, etc.) — add push-to-Supabase after local writes

## Downstream Consumer: Telegram Bot (Story #54)
The renamed Supabase tables are also consumed by an external Telegram bot (story #54) that writes tasks directly to Supabase using a service role key. Ensure:
- The renamed tables (`projects`, `tasks`, `statuses`, `project_members`, `user_labels`) support inserts from the service role key (bypasses RLS)
- `task_labels` junction table is writable so the bot can assign labels
- The `user_labels` table supports label lookup by name + user_id so the bot can resolve or auto-create labels
- Realtime subscriptions on the app side pick up tasks created by the bot immediately

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: All personal project data (tasks, statuses, labels, task_labels, activity_log) syncs to Supabase automatically on every write
User-level data (settings, themes, saved views, project areas) syncs to Supabase automatically
New device login pulls all data from Supabase and populates local SQLite
Supabase shared_* tables renamed to generic names; all SyncService references updated
New user-scoped Supabase tables created with RLS policies
Realtime subscriptions deliver remote changes for all projects (not just shared)
Offline detection via Supabase Realtime connection state with navigator.onLine fallback
Personal projects remain editable offline; writes queued and flushed on reconnect
Shared projects become read-only when offline with visual banner
Sync status icon next to app title: green (synced), spinning (syncing), orange (offline)
Click on sync icon shows tooltip with last sync time
First-time sync shows progress indicator
Deletes propagate across devices
Existing shared project collaboration (invites, member management, Realtime) still works
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Sidebar Customization
stories.description:
Allow users to show/hide and reorder sidebar navigation items, move Stats to the footer, replace the sidebar collapse toggle with a light/dark mode toggle, and remove sidebar collapse functionality entirely.

## Sidebar Layout Changes
- Remove sidebar collapse/expand functionality — sidebar is always expanded
- Remove the collapse/expand button next to the app title in the top-left corner
- Replace it with a light/dark mode toggle using iOS-style sun/moon icons
- Remove the dark/light mode toggle from the sidebar footer
- Move Stats from the top navigation to the footer (next to Help, Settings, MCP). Clicking Stats still opens the Stats view in the main content area (same behavior as now)

## Toggleable Items (show/hide via Settings → General → Sidebar)
- Calendar, Stats, Views, Archive, Templates
- My Day is always visible (not toggleable)
- Footer items (Help, Settings, MCP) are always visible, not toggleable or reorderable

## Reorderable Items (drag in Settings → General → Sidebar)
- My Day, Calendar, Views, Archive, Templates
- Drag handles to reorder, live preview

## Dynamic Keyboard Shortcuts
- Shortcuts (⌘1, ⌘2, ⌘3...) are assigned by visible position order
- Hidden items have no shortcut
- Reordering changes which view gets which shortcut
- Example: if user reorders Calendar first, My Day second → ⌘1=Calendar, ⌘2=My Day

## Settings UI (Settings → General → Sidebar section)
- List of sidebar items with drag handles + on/off toggles
- Changes apply immediately (live preview)

## Data Storage
- `sidebar_order` setting: JSON array of view IDs in display order
- `sidebar_hidden` setting: JSON array of hidden view IDs
- Defaults = current layout, nothing hidden

## Key Files
- `src/renderer/src/features/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` — read settings, render conditionally, dynamic shortcuts
- `src/renderer/src/features/settings/GeneralSettingsContent.tsx` — new Sidebar section with reorder + toggle UI
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/settingsStore.ts` — read sidebar_order and sidebar_hidden settings

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Calendar, Stats, Views, Archive, and Templates can be toggled on/off in Settings → General → Sidebar
Sidebar items can be reordered via drag in Settings → General → Sidebar
My Day is always visible and cannot be hidden
Keyboard shortcuts (⌘1, ⌘2, etc.) dynamically follow visible position order
Hidden items have no keyboard shortcut assigned
Stats appears in the sidebar footer next to Help/Settings/MCP; clicking opens Stats view
Light/dark mode toggle (iOS-style sun/moon icons) replaces the collapse button next to the app title in top-left
Dark/light mode toggle removed from sidebar footer
Sidebar collapse/expand functionality removed entirely — sidebar is always expanded
Configuration persisted via sidebar_order and sidebar_hidden settings keys
Default layout matches current sidebar when no settings exist
Footer items (Help, Settings, MCP) are always visible
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Full Task Rows in Saved Views + Multi-Sort
stories.description:
Replace the minimal SavedViewTaskRow with the full TaskRow component so saved views show all task information (status, labels, priority, due date, assignee, timer, subtasks, recurrence). Add multi-sort to both saved views and project views, with drag-to-reorder as "Custom" sort.

## Saved Views — Full TaskRow
- Replace `SavedViewTaskRow` in `SavedViewListView.tsx` with the full `TaskRow` component
- Each row shows: project indicator, status button, title, labels, priority badge, due date, recurrence, timer, subtask count, assignee (for shared projects)
- Context menu, inline editing, bulk selection all work
- No drag-to-reorder (not applicable for cross-project views)

## Sidebar Saved View Icons
- Each saved view item in the sidebar shows a small colored dot using the view's `color` field
- Consistent with how projects show color dots in the sidebar
- When creating a new saved view, auto-assign the next available color using the same cycle logic as labels and projects
- Color is editable via the saved view's context menu (right-click → Change Color) using the same color picker as projects/labels

## Multi-Sort
- Sort button in the filter bar (next to existing filter controls), opens a dropdown
- Sort rules are an ordered array: `[{field: "priority", direction: "desc"}, {field: "due_date", direction: "asc"}]`
- Available fields: Priority, Due date, Status, Created, Updated, Title, Project, Custom (project views only)
- Each rule shows field + direction toggle (↑/↓) + remove button
- "+ Add sort" button to stack multiple rules
- "Custom" sort = manual drag order (order_index) — only available in project views

## Sort in Project Views
- Same sort dropdown in the filter bar
- Default sort: "Custom" (current drag-to-reorder behavior)
- When sort is anything other than "Custom", drag-to-reorder is disabled (handles hidden/greyed out)
- Switching back to "Custom" re-enables drag
- Migrate `priority_auto_sort` setting: if `true`, set project sort to `[{field: "priority", direction: "desc"}]` and remove the old setting

## Sort Persistence
- Saved views: `sort` array stored in `filter_config` JSON alongside filters
- Project views: `sort_config_{projectId}` settings key per user
- Shared projects: sort preference is per-user (not synced to other members)
- Default: project views = `custom asc`, saved views = `priority desc`

## Sort UI
- Sort button shows current primary sort as label (e.g., "Priority ↓")
- Dropdown shows all active sort rules with field picker, direction toggle, remove button
- "+ Add sort" at bottom
- "Clear sort" resets to default

## Key Files
- `src/renderer/src/features/views/SavedViewListView.tsx` — replace SavedViewTaskRow with TaskRow, apply sort
- `src/renderer/src/shared/components/FilterBar.tsx` — add Sort button + dropdown
- `src/renderer/src/features/tasks/TaskListView.tsx` — integrate sort, disable drag when non-Custom
- `src/renderer/src/features/tasks/TaskRow.tsx` — no changes needed (already full-featured)
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/labelStore.ts` — add sort state

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Saved views use the full TaskRow component with all task information visible
Saved views show project indicator on each task row
Context menu, inline editing, and bulk selection work in saved views
Drag-to-reorder is not available in saved views
Multi-sort dropdown available in filter bar for both saved views and project views
Sort fields: Priority, Due date, Status, Created, Updated, Title, Project, Custom
Multiple sort rules can be stacked (e.g., Priority then Due date)
Each sort rule has field picker, direction toggle, and remove button
Custom sort only available in project views; enables drag-to-reorder
Non-Custom sort in project views disables drag-to-reorder (handles hidden)
Sort persisted per saved view in filter_config JSON
Sort persisted per project view in settings (per-user for shared projects)
priority_auto_sort setting migrated to new sort system
Default sort: Custom for project views, Priority desc for saved views
Saved view items in sidebar show a colored dot using the view's color field
New saved views auto-assign the next available color (same cycle logic as labels/projects)
Saved view color is editable via context menu using the same color picker as projects/labels
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: ToDoozy Telegram Bot
stories.description:
A Node.js Telegram bot running as a Docker container on a Raspberry Pi (deployed via Portainer). Uses the same smart input parser syntax as the in-app quick-add to create tasks via Supabase. Also supports listing project tasks, viewing My Day, and completing tasks.

## Architecture
- `packages/telegram-bot/` in the ToDoozy repo
- Node.js + TypeScript + `node-telegram-bot-api` + `@supabase/supabase-js`
- Shares smart input parser logic from `src/renderer/src/shared/hooks/smartInputParser.ts` (extracted or copied)
- Connects directly to Supabase using service role key
- Docker container deployed to Pi via Portainer MCP

## Task Creation (plain text message)
Uses the same syntax as the in-app quick-add:
- `@label` — assign label (auto-create if doesn't exist, using same color cycle logic)
- `/project` — assign to project (when mixed with other text)
- `d:today`, `d:tomorrow`, `d:monday`, `d:2026-04-10` — due date
- `p:high`, `p:3` — priority
- `r:https://url.com` — reference URL
- `s:status` — status
- Everything else = task title

Example: `eggs and milk /groceries d:today @fast r:www.delhaize.com`

Reply confirmation:
```
✅ eggs and milk
📁 Groceries · 📅 Today · 🏷 fast
🔗 delhaize.com
```

## List Project Tasks (`/projectname` standalone)
When message is only `/projectname` with no other text, lists all non-done tasks in that project with inline buttons to mark done.

```
📁 Groceries (3 tasks)

○ eggs and milk · 📅 Today
○ bread
○ check pantry · p:low

[✓ eggs and milk] [✓ bread] [✓ check pantry]
```

Tapping a button completes the task in Supabase and updates the message.

## Complete Tasks (`/done`)
- `/done` alone — shows recent non-done tasks across all projects with inline buttons to complete
- `/done <text>` — fuzzy-matches title, completes immediately

## My Day (`/myday`)
Lists all My Day tasks with inline done buttons.

## Authentication
- `ALLOWED_TELEGRAM_IDS` env var — only responds to whitelisted Telegram user IDs (674103800)
- `TODOOZY_USER_ID` env var — Supabase user ID to create tasks as
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` — service role key to bypass RLS

## Deployment
- `Dockerfile` (Node.js Alpine, arm64 compatible for Raspberry Pi)
- `docker-compose.yml` with env vars
- Deploy via Portainer `createLocalStack` MCP tool

## Env Vars
- `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`: `8650981704:AAHvMGGYthdzVZ7GlnIKst2VOu1QebRF-I8`
- `SUPABASE_URL`: from .env
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`: service role key
- `TODOOZY_USER_ID`: user's Supabase auth.uid()
- `ALLOWED_TELEGRAM_IDS`: `674103800`

## Depends On
Story #51 (Supabase Full Sync Engine) — the renamed Supabase tables must be in place for the bot to write to.

## Key Files
- `packages/telegram-bot/src/index.ts` — bot entry point, message handler
- `packages/telegram-bot/src/parser.ts` — smart input parser (shared or copied from smartInputParser.ts)
- `packages/telegram-bot/src/supabase.ts` — Supabase client + task/label/project CRUD
- `packages/telegram-bot/Dockerfile`
- `packages/telegram-bot/docker-compose.yml`

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Plain text messages create tasks using smart input parser syntax (@label, /project, d:date, p:priority, r:url)
Bot replies with formatted confirmation showing parsed title, project, labels, due date, priority, URL
/projectname standalone lists all non-done tasks in that project
Task list shows inline buttons to mark tasks done
Tapping done button completes the task in Supabase and updates the message
/done shows recent non-done tasks with inline buttons
/done <text> fuzzy-matches and completes immediately
/myday lists My Day tasks with done buttons
Bot only responds to whitelisted Telegram user IDs
Labels auto-created if they don't exist (using same auto-color cycle logic)
Projects fuzzy-matched by name
Docker container runs on Raspberry Pi via Portainer
Bot token and credentials stored as env vars, not hardcoded in source
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests
